meta.name: HADS Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale
meta.description: The HADS is a 14-item self-report scale designed to detect anxiety and depression in medical outpatient settings. It avoids somatic symptoms that might be confused with physical illness, making it particularly suitable for hospital patients.
meta.author: Zigmond & Snaith (1983)
meta.reference: Zigmond AS, Snaith RP. The Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale. Acta Psychiatr Scand. 1983;67(6):361-370.
meta.instruction: Read each item and select the reply which comes closest to how you have been feeling in the past week. Your immediate reaction to each item will probably be more accurate than a long thought-out response.

questions.text: I feel tense or wound up
I still enjoy the things I used to enjoy
I get a sort of frightened feeling as if something awful is about to happen

choices.label: A little, but it does not worry me

questions.text: I can laugh and see the funny side of things

choices.label: As much as I always could

questions.text: Worrying thoughts go through my mind

choices.label: A great deal of the time

questions.text: I can sit at ease and feel relaxed
I feel as if I am slowed down
I get a sort of frightened feeling like butterflies in the stomach
I have lost interest in my appearance

choices.label: I do not take as much care as I should
I may not take quite as much care
I take just as much care as ever

questions.text: I feel restless as if I have to be on the move
I look forward with enjoyment to things

choices.label: As much as I ever did
Rather less than I used to
Definitely less than I used to

questions.text: I get sudden feelings of panic
I can enjoy a good book or radio or TV program

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your combined anxiety and depression scores are within the normal range, suggesting no significant emotional distress. Review your individual subscale scores below for a more specific picture of your anxiety and depression levels.
suggestions: Review individual subscale scores for more detail

ranges.label: Borderline
ranges.description: Your combined score suggests some emotional distress that may warrant monitoring. Review your anxiety and depression subscale scores to identify which area contributes more to your overall score, as targeted intervention is more effective.
suggestions: Review subscale scores to identify the primary area of concern
Monitor your symptoms over the coming weeks
Practice stress management and self-care activities
Consider consulting a healthcare professional if symptoms persist

ranges.label: Significant Distress
ranges.description: Your combined score indicates significant emotional distress in anxiety and/or depression. At this level, professional evaluation is recommended. The HADS is widely used in medical settings and elevated scores may reflect psychological responses to health conditions as well as standalone mental health concerns.
suggestions: Consult your healthcare provider for evaluation and appropriate referral
Review your subscale scores to understand whether anxiety, depression, or both are contributing
Evidence-based treatments are effective for both hospital-related and general anxiety and depression

anxiety.label: Normal
anxiety.description: Your anxiety subscale score is within the normal range. You do not appear to be experiencing clinically significant anxiety symptoms in the context of your health or daily life.

anxiety.label: Borderline
anxiety.description: Your anxiety score is in the borderline range, suggesting some anxiety symptoms that warrant monitoring. You may be experiencing worry, tension, or restlessness that is noticeable but not yet severely impairing. Further assessment may be helpful.

anxiety.label: Clinical Anxiety
anxiety.description: Your anxiety subscale score is in the clinical range, indicating significant anxiety symptoms. You may be experiencing persistent worry, panic, tension, or fear that is interfering with your daily functioning. Professional evaluation and treatment are recommended.

depression.label: Normal
depression.description: Your depression subscale score is within the normal range. You do not appear to be experiencing significant depressive symptoms such as loss of interest, reduced pleasure, or persistent low mood.

depression.label: Borderline
depression.description: Your depression score is in the borderline range, suggesting some depressive symptoms that warrant monitoring. You may notice reduced enjoyment of activities, slowed thinking, or a general sense of not feeling yourself. Follow-up assessment may be beneficial.

depression.label: Clinical Depression
depression.description: Your depression subscale score is in the clinical range, indicating significant depressive symptoms. You may be experiencing loss of interest in activities, persistent low mood, or feelings of hopelessness. Professional evaluation and treatment are recommended.
